title: Irish Braise Stew
description: A hearty and comforting Irish stew made with tender beef and flavorful root vegetables, all in a rich and peppery broth.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the beef and cook until browned, about 5 minutes. Remove from heat and set aside.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the chopped carrots and potatoes to the pot and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Add the sliced mushrooms to the pot and cook until they release their liquid and start to brown, about 5 minutes.
Add the browned beef back to the pot, along with the beef broth, peppercorns, and milk.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then cover the pot and transfer it to the oven.
Braise the stew in the oven for 2-3 hours, or until the beef is tender and the vegetables are cooked through.
Remove the pot from the oven and stir in the gluten-free flour to thicken the stew.
Season the stew with salt and pepper to taste, then serve hot.
